skill.name: azure-postgres
skill.description: Configure Microsoft Entra ID authentication for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Set up developer, group, and managed identity access, apply PostgreSQL permissions, and plan password-to-Entra migrations.
skill.summary: Configure passwordless Azure PostgreSQL access with Microsoft Entra ID.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, credential, and heuristic matches. Most matches in Markdown references are false positives from documentation links, inline command names, and PostgreSQL SECURITY LABEL syntax. The real risk is moderate: included shell scripts execute Azure CLI and psql administration commands, export database access tokens, and build SQL from caller-provided identifiers.

medium_findings.title: Administrative scripts execute Azure and PostgreSQL commands
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Bash scripts that call Azure CLI and psql to inspect servers, create Entra-mapped roles, change server parameters, and grant database permissions. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it can change cloud and database authorization state when run by an operator with privileges. Static external command findings are true positives for the scripts and false positives for most Markdown examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly invoke az and psql and perform role or permission administration. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the operational impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: SQL constructed from caller-provided identifiers
medium_findings.description: Several scripts insert resource names, database names, user names, group names, or identity names into SQL statements. Shell arguments are generally quoted for command invocation, but SQL identifiers and string values are not consistently validated or escaped before execution as an Entra admin.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines show variables embedded in SQL passed to psql. Legitimate inputs are expected, but malicious or malformed identifiers could alter SQL behavior.

medium_findings.title: Database access tokens are stored in environment variables
medium_findings.description: The scripts acquire an Azure Database for PostgreSQL access token and place it in PGPASSWORD for psql. This is a standard psql pattern, but environment-based token handling can expose credentials to child processes or local diagnostics if the operator environment is compromised.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token assignment is explicit and repeated. It is a common operational pattern, so the concern is exposure risk rather than malicious handling.

low_findings.title: Documentation patterns triggered false positives
low_findings.description: Many static findings come from Markdown code spans, relative documentation links, command examples, and PostgreSQL SECURITY LABEL syntax. No evidence found that these reference files execute code, access local files, or target the Windows SAM database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation or SQL reference text. It explains Azure PostgreSQL operations and does not create an execution path by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: SQL constructed from caller-provided identifiers
dangerous_patterns.description: Several scripts insert resource names, database names, user names, group names, or identity names into SQL statements. Shell arguments are generally quoted for command invocation, but SQL identifiers and string values are not consistently validated or escaped before execution as an Entra admin.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines show variables embedded in SQL passed to psql. Legitimate inputs are expected, but malicious or malformed identifiers could alter SQL behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database access tokens are stored in environment variables
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts acquire an Azure Database for PostgreSQL access token and place it in PGPASSWORD for psql. This is a standard psql pattern, but environment-based token handling can expose credentials to child processes or local diagnostics if the operator environment is compromised.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token assignment is explicit and repeated. It is a common operational pattern, so the concern is exposure risk rather than malicious handling.

content.value_statement: PostgreSQL teams need secure database access without shared passwords or static connection secrets. This skill guides Entra ID setup, managed identity access, group permissions, and migration planning for Azure PostgreSQL.
actual_capabilities: Explains Entra ID authentication setup for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
Provides Bash scripts for user, group, managed identity, and migration workflows.
Documents Azure CLI commands for Entra administrators, tokens, identity lookup, and server parameters.
Provides PostgreSQL SQL function examples for Entra role creation and group sync.
Includes troubleshooting guidance for token, role, username, DNS, and firewall issues.
limitations: Requires an Azure subscription and appropriate permissions for PostgreSQL and Entra ID.
Scripts assume Azure CLI, jq, psql, and a trusted local shell environment.
Does not validate every database identifier before SQL execution.
Does not replace a production change plan or access review process.

use_cases.title: Add Developer Database Access
use_cases.description: Grant a developer read-only, read-write, or admin access through their Entra ID user account.

use_cases.title: Connect Applications Without Secrets
use_cases.description: Map a managed identity to a PostgreSQL role so an Azure-hosted app can connect without stored passwords.

use_cases.title: Move Teams to Group-Based Access
use_cases.description: Use Entra groups to manage PostgreSQL permissions and decide whether group member sync is required.

prompt_templates.title: Check Server Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me check whether my Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server is ready for Microsoft Entra ID authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Grant User Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through granting {user_upn} {permission_level} access to database {database_name} on server {server_name}.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Managed Identity
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan managed identity access for app identity {identity_name} to Azure PostgreSQL database {database_name} with least privilege.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Password Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staged migration plan from password authentication to Entra-only authentication for Azure PostgreSQL server {server_name}.

output_examples.input: Help me add a developer with read-only access.
output: Confirms the server, database, and Entra admin prerequisites.
Identifies the developer principal and creates the mapped PostgreSQL role.
Applies connect, schema usage, and table read permissions.
Gives a validation checklist for token-based connection testing.

output_examples.input: Set up managed identity access for my web app.
output: Looks up the managed identity object and client identifiers.
Creates or reuses a PostgreSQL role for the service principal.
Grants only the requested database permission level.
Summarizes the connection settings the application needs.
Checks token freshness, username format, and mapped role existence.
Separates database permission problems from network or firewall failures.
Recommends exact follow-up checks for Entra admin and group sync state.

best_practices: Test changes in a non-production database before changing production authentication mode.
Grant the smallest permission level needed for each user, group, or managed identity.
Review generated SQL and Azure commands before running scripts with administrator privileges.
anti_patterns: Do not pass untrusted names or database identifiers directly into the included scripts.
Do not grant azure_pg_admin to application identities unless there is a documented requirement.
Do not disable password authentication until every migrated role has been tested with Entra ID.

faq.question: What does this skill configure?
faq.answer: It configures Microsoft Entra ID authentication and database permissions for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server.

faq.question: Does it create PostgreSQL servers?
faq.answer: It includes a basic create command reference, but its main focus is authentication and access configuration.

faq.question: Can it support managed identities?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a managed identity workflow that maps the identity to a PostgreSQL role.

faq.question: Can it manage team access?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents Entra group roles, group sync modes, and group permission grants.

faq.question: What tools must be installed for scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts expect Azure CLI, jq, psql, Bash, and an authenticated Azure session.

faq.question: Is it safe to run scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. Review all parameters and SQL before running scripts because they can change database permissions.
